title: How was your weekend?
setting: It's Monday morning. Your coworker wants to hear about your weekend.

steps.speaker: Compañero
steps.en: What did you do over the weekend?

options.es: Fui a la playa con unos amigos.
options.en: I went to the beach with some friends.
options.feedback: Past tense nailed — 'fui' (I went) is the one to know. Great.
That's present tense — sounds like a plan, not what happened. He asked about the past.
He'll get the gist, but a full sentence shows off your past tense.

options.en: It was a lot of fun. I swam a lot.
options.feedback: 'Estuvo' and 'nadé' — two past forms in one breath. You're getting it.
Present tense again — try 'estuvo' (it was) for something that's done.

options.en: Lots of fun, I ate fish.
options.feedback: Nice — 'comí' (I ate) is perfect. Casual and clear.

options.es: Descansé en casa y vi una película.
options.en: I rested at home and watched a movie.
options.feedback: Two more clean past verbs. You're telling a real story now.
Short but correct — 'descansé' is spot on. Totally natural.
That's a habit (present). He asked about THIS Sunday — try 'descansé.'

steps.speaker: Compañero
steps.es: Suena a un buen fin de semana.

options.es: Sí, la verdad estuvo bien. ¿Y el tuyo?
options.en: Yeah, honestly it was good. And yours?
options.feedback: Perfect close — you answered AND turned it back to him. Real conversation.
Fine — though asking about his weekend would keep it flowing.
A bit of a conversation-ender. Toss the question back to him!
